2603.22900.title: Off-Policy Evaluation and Learning for Survival Outcomes under Censoring
2603.22900.paper_summary: Optimizing survival outcomes, such as patient survival or customer retention, is a critical objective in data-driven decision-making. Off-Policy Evaluation~(OPE) provides a powerful framework for assessing such decision-making policies using logged data alone, without the need for costly or risky online experiments in high-stakes applications. However, typical estimators are not designed to handle right-censored survival outcomes, as they ignore unobserved survival times beyond the censoring time, leading to systematic underestimation of the true policy performance. To address this issue, we propose a novel framework for OPE and Off-Policy Learning~(OPL) tailored for survival outcomes under censoring. Specifically, we introduce IPCW-IPS and IPCW-DR, which employ the Inverse Probability of Censoring Weighting technique to explicitly deal with censoring bias. We theoretically establish that our estimators are unbiased and that IPCW-DR achieves double robustness, ensuring consistency if either the propensity score or the outcome model is correct. Furthermore, we extend this framework to constrained OPL to optimize policy value under budget constraints. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed methods through simulation studies and illustrate their practical impacts using public real-world data for both evaluation and learning tasks.
2603.22900.paper_summary_zh: 優化生存結果，例如患者生存或客戶保留，是數據驅動決策中的一個關鍵目標。離線政策評估（OPE）提供了一個強大的框架，用於僅使用記錄數據來評估這些決策政策，而無需在高風險應用中進行昂貴或危險的在線實驗。然而，典型的估計器並未設計用於處理右截尾的生存結果，因為它們忽略了超過截尾時間的未觀察生存時間，導致對真實政策表現的系統性低估。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個針對生存結果在截尾下的OPE和離線政策學習（OPL）的新框架。具體而言，我們引入了IPCW-IPS和IPCW-DR，這些方法採用了逆概率截尾加權技術，以明確處理截尾偏差。我們從理論上證明了我們的估計器是無偏的，並且IPCW-DR實現了雙重穩健性，確保如果傾向得分或結果模型中的任一個是正確的，則保持一致性。此外，我們將這一框架擴展到受限的OPL，以在預算約束下優化政策價值。我們通過模擬研究展示了我們提出的方法的有效性，並使用公共現實世界數據說明了它們在評估和學習任務中的實際影響。
2603.22900.author: Kohsuke Kubota et.al.
2603.22900.authors: Kohsuke Kubota, Mitsuhiro Takahashi, Yuta Saito
